notes: Bridge map for model theorem -> Lean conformance evidence. Each critical_op carries one evidence_level from the exact mixed taxonomy; Go/Rust correspondence remains human-reviewed rather than a machine-checked implementation-equivalence claim.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Bounded Go-trace replay over exactly the listed generated parse_tx vectors.
limitations: The trace covers only PARSE-01 and PARSE-16 from the current generated source.
It does not establish complete parse-stage coverage or cross-language refinement for arbitrary bytes.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Bounded Go-trace replay for exactly the listed current sighash_v1 vectors, with structural selector/base-type model support.
limitations: The executable replay covers the current CV-SIGHASH fixture set, not arbitrary raw transaction / witness-carried sighash_type combinations.
DA kinds lack an active Lean live bridge; this row does not claim live Go/Rust DigestV1 correspondence or a full-protocol residual.
critical_ops.notes: Bounded Go-trace contract for the structural selector/base-type surface; it is not a live implementation bridge.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Bounded CV-POW replay contract for retargetV1, supported by a behavioral decomposition of the three-way clamp with constant pins, candidate monotonicity, 4x stability boundary, lo≤hi under valid preconditions, strengthened clamped range, and timestamp clamping.
limitations: The model theorem is bounded CV replay plus behavioral support; it does not claim a universal end-to-end refinement theorem for every retargetV1 input tuple.
The trace binding is limited to the exact generated retarget_v1 IDs listed below.
critical_ops.notes: Behavioral closure of §15 retarget formula. The live retargetV1 function is validated against CV-POW vectors (native_decide), and the three-way clamp formula is decomposed into pure functions with full constant pins, candidate monotonicity (both tActual and targetOldNat), lo≤hi proof, strengthened clamped range [lo,hi], and 4x stability boundary. Helper lemma nat_div_le_div_right proved from Nat.div_add_mod since Lean 4.6.0 Init lacks Nat.div_le_div_right.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Bounded contract over exactly six positive protocol-generated CV-FORK-CHOICE vectors with op=fork_choice_select, replayed against selectWinner. Their order is pinned by forkChoiceSelectExpectedIds; the generator excludes expect_ok=false rows.
limitations: Generated contract over exactly six current positive CV-FORK-CHOICE fork_choice_select vectors, not a universal proof for all possible chain configurations.
Negative expect_ok=false vectors are runtime conformance-only because the protocol generator excludes them; standalone-only extra vectors are not claimed by this row.
Data source is the protocol-generated CV-FORK-CHOICE Lean vectors, not Go trace (go_trace_v1.jsonl does not contain fork_choice_select operations).
Equivalence with Go/Rust implementation is structural (same if/else chain), not proved via rfl.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Bounded Go-trace replay for exactly the listed utxo_apply_basic vectors, supported by separately registered UTXO conservation and anti-double-spend theorems.
limitations: The trace contract covers only the listed vectors. Separate universal theorems cover `connectBlockTxs` (non-coinbase transaction processing); header linkage, PoW, and coinbase checks are proved elsewhere.
kernel_checked records no Lean.ofReduceBool compiler/evaluator dependency and does not assert absence of ordinary Lean foundations.

critical_ops.notes: Universal closure of the live DA validator surface. validateDASetIntegrity_ok_constrained decomposes .ok into accumulateDATxs + batch count bound + orphan check + verifyCommitIntegrity with parse-derived witnesses. validateWitnessErrors is extracted as a LIVE sub-function; the full DA pipeline is decomposed into recursive helpers plus gate-level error propagation, with CV-DA-INTEGRITY replay backing the same surface.
critical_ops.contract_scope: Universal closure of S21 DA set integrity. Constrained theorem decomposes .ok into all 4 stages with parse-derived witnesses.

Universal helper-level accept/reject equivalence: given one identical precheck result and signature-task list, sequential reduction and the live SigCheckQueue.Flush accept/reject contract return the same verdict.
limitations: No theorem composes this helper result with `connectBlockFullComputed*` into end-to-end sequential-versus-parallel block equality. The caller-supplied ConnectBlockFull wrapper lemmas are experimental and unregistered.

Auxiliary tagged-result lowest-index helper theorems are excluded from this bridge because the live queue promises deterministic submission-order surfacing under early abort, not a universal absolute-lowest-failing-index contract.
critical_ops.notes: The row is intentionally limited to the existing live queue helper theorem. Retained ConnectBlockFull computed/wrapper lemmas are experimental model facts, not parallel-composition evidence.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Universal closure of S9 weight formula. Full Except-chain proof: txWeightAndStats_ok_weight_eq_constrained records the successful parseTxHeader + parseTxBody + finalizeTxWeight chain and proves .ok implies the returned weight matches computeWeight on parse-derived witnesses.
critical_ops.notes: Universal closure of §9 weight formula. The live txWeightAndStats path now has a parse-constrained full-chain theorem plus a non-vacuous positivity guard; CV-WEIGHT remains the executable evidence lane, and WeightSuiteAware.lean supporting theorems close the suite-aware post-rotation cost model within the same section-level universal row.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Behavioral state-machine model of §23.2.1 descriptor activation lifecycle proving at-most-one uniqueness (inductive), five-phase exhaustive height partition with mutual exclusion. Conformance: 11 vectors (6 cutoff + 5 sunset) structurally replayed.
limitations: Behavioral model only. No Go/Rust implementation bridge is claimed; the phase functions are Lean definitions rather than extracted production code.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Universal closure of §23 feature activation FSM / prechecked state-only state-at-height fold surface. featurebit_state_at_height_from_window_counts_state_eq_fold bridges the live multi-boundary state helper to the fold-based FSM model after deployment validation (`startHeight ≤ timeoutHeight`) and the sufficient window_signal_counts prefix guard, and featurebit_state_at_height_from_window_counts_state_monotone lifts multi_step_monotone onto that reachable live height path.
critical_ops.notes: Universal closure of the §23 feature activation FSM state surface on the reachable prechecked state-at-height fold path. Existing single-step transition and flagday theorems remain complementary support.
limitations: The state-only bridge applies only after live deployment validation (`startHeight ≤ timeoutHeight`) and sufficient window_signal_counts. Invalid deployments and the helper's count-prefix error branch reject before the modeled fold.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Universal closure of the descriptor-aware P2PK spend-side live gate bridge. Under the same preceding live structural guards, validateP2PKSpendPreSig and the P2PK branch of scanSingleInputStep succeed iff the committed suite is in NativeSpendSuites(h,d); inactive suites reject with the exact live error codes.
critical_ops.notes: Universal closure of the live post-rotation spend gate bridge on the descriptor-aware P2PK spend path. The counted closure is the direct spend helper plus the input-scan gate; FI-ROT-04/FI-ROT-06 model theorems remain supporting evidence rather than the counted closure itself.
limitations: Scope: descriptor-aware P2PK spend-side live gate bridge only; create-side gate and other spend validators are tracked separately.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Universal for native crypto suite rotation. Constrained theorem: ∀ well-formed descriptor+registry, ∀ height → phase partition holds ∧ all active create/spend suites resolve uniquely.
critical_ops.notes: Universal for the native suite rotation model surface. Composes fi_rot_02_phase_partition + fi_rot_03_active_{create,suite}_resolves. No claim about byte-level wire encoding trace (G7 residual).
limitations: The model is machine-checked in Lean; correspondence to Go/Rust is human-reviewed, and byte-level wire encoding trace remains unformalized (G7 residual).
